title: Why Self-Attention Captures Context
concept: Self-attention computes relations directly between any two words, bypassing the step-by-step decay limits of recurrent models.

exercises.prompt: What is the main goal of the self-attention mechanism?

options.text: To translate words into different languages instantly
To find relationships between words in a sentence to capture context
To check the spelling of words in a paragraph

exercises.explain: Self-attention allows models to look at all parts of a sentence simultaneously to understand how words relate to each other.

cards.title: Lost in the Middle
cards.body: Have you ever read a long sentence and forgotten how it started by the time you reached the end? Older AI models did the exact same thing! Self-attention was invented to solve this memory problem.

cards.title: What is Self-Attention?
cards.body: Self-attention is a mechanism that allows a machine learning model to calculate connections between any two words in a sentence, no matter how far apart they are. Instead of reading step-by-step, it looks at the whole sentence at once.

cards.title: The Telephone Game vs. The Open Room
cards.body: Imagine trying to pass a secret message down a long line of students (Recurrent Networks) versus letting everyone in the room look at and talk to each other directly at the same time (Self-Attention). In the line, the message gets distorted by the end. With self-attention, there is no game of telephone—everyone connects instantly!

cards.title: Context in Action
cards.body:
In the sentence: "The bank of the river had a lot of clay, so the bank denied my loan application." 

A self-attention model connects the first "bank" with "river" and "clay", while connecting the second "bank" with "loan" and "denied". It figures out two different meanings instantly by looking at the surrounding context words!

cards.title: Distance Doesn't Matter
cards.body: Many believe that self-attention can only connect words that are right next to each other. In reality, a word at the very start of a book can pay attention to a word at the very end just as easily as to its next-door neighbor!

cards.title: How Information Flows
cards.body: See how different architectures handle connections across a sentence.

left.text: Step-by-step chain. Information decays and fades away over long distances.

right.text: Direct web. Every word connects instantly to every other word.

exercises.prompt: Why do older recurrent models struggle with long-distance context?

options.text: They only look at the punctuation marks.
They are too fast at reading sentences.
They process words step-by-step, causing early details to fade.
They completely ignore the middle words.

exercises.explain: Recurrent models process words sequentially (one by one), which causes early details to fade away before reaching the end of a long sentence.

exercises.prompt: True or False: Self-attention requires processing the first word completely before it can look at the last word.
exercises.explain: Self-attention allows the model to look at all words at the same time, calculating relationships in parallel.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the blank to describe how self-attention connects words.
exercises.sentence: Self-attention bypasses step-by-step decay by creating [blank] connections between all words in a sentence.
exercises.explain: Self-attention makes direct connections between all words, so it doesn't lose information over long distances like older models did.

exercises.prompt: We test three AI models on the sentence: "The dog ran across the busy street because it was scared." Which model is using self-attention correctly to capture context?

options.text: AI Model A: Connects "it" to "street" because "street" is the closest noun.
AI Model B: Connects "it" directly to "dog" even though they are far apart.
AI Model C: Processes "it" without linking it to any other word.

exercises.explain: AI Model B uses self-attention to link "it" directly to "dog", showing a correct understanding of context. AI Model A incorrectly links "it" to "street" because it was the closest noun. AI Model C has no connection at all.

exercises.prompt: Why does self-attention capture long-range context much better than older recurrent models?

options.text: It processes words one by one and forgets the beginning of long sentences.
It connects every word directly to every other word, bypassing step-by-step decay.
It limits the focus to only neighboring words in a sentence.
It converts text into images so the computer can see the context.

exercises.explain: Self-attention allows every word in a sequence to connect directly with every other word in a single step, bypassing the sequential step-by-step decay of older networks.
